turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Hi, thank you for having me. I'm Alex, nice to meet you.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Welcome, Alex. Could you start by telling me a bit about yourself and what brought you here today?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Sure. I'm a former high school math teacher looking to move into data analytics. I finished a bootcamp recently and I'm excited about this opportunity.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Thanks for sharing. Can you walk me through your most recent role and what you were responsible for there?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: My most recent professional role was teaching high school math. I taught Algebra II and Statistics, designed lesson plans, and tracked student performance data.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Got it. How many years of experience do you have using SQL, which I see listed as your top skill?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I've used SQL intensively for about a year now, mainly through my bootcamp and personal projects. Before that, I used basic database queries occasionally while teaching.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Nice. I noticed on your resume a project called Nashville Housing Market: Price Drivers and Foreclosure Risk. Can you tell me about that?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes. I pulled public housing data, cleaned it in SQL, and built a model in Python to identify price drivers and flag foreclosure risk. I visualized the results in Tableau.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: That sounds relevant. What would you say was your biggest achievement in your most recent role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I'd say redesigning our Statistics curriculum to include more real-data projects. Student engagement and test scores both improved noticeably that year.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Great. Why are you interested in this Data Analyst role at Northwind Logistics specifically?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Northwind's logistics focus means interesting supply chain data, and I like that you're a growing company. It feels like a place where I can add value while learning fast.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Understood. You mentioned earlier that you have relevant analytics experience from projects and your bootcamp. Can you expand on that?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Absolutely. I completed an eight-month data analytics bootcamp covering SQL, Python, Tableau, and statistics. I also built two end-to-end projects, including the Nashville housing one.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: I also want to address something I noticed. There's a roughly two-year gap on your resume after your teaching career. Can you walk me through that?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Of course. After teaching, I took time off to care for a family member who was ill. Once that stabilized, I decided to pursue the career switch and enrolled in the bootcamp.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Thank you for being open about that. Before we go further, could you tell me about your educational background?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: I have a Bachelor's in Mathematics and a teaching credential. I recently completed a Data Analytics bootcamp certificate as well.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Great. Can you share a specific challenge you faced and how you handled it, using the STAR format?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Sure. In my bootcamp capstone, our team had a member go silent. I stepped in to redistribute the workload, reorganized our timeline, and we delivered the project on time with a strong final presentation.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Appreciate that. What are your salary expectations for this role?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Based on my research for entry-level data analyst roles in this area, I'd be targeting somewhere in the 65 to 75 thousand range, though I'm open to discussion.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Good to know. Do you have any questions for me about the role or the team?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Yes, what does a typical day look like for this role, and how large is the analytics team I'd be joining?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: It's a team of six. Going back to your bootcamp capstone, how long did that project take from start to finish?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: The capstone ran for about six weeks total, with the final two weeks dedicated to analysis and presentation.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Quick one: do you have a valid driver's license?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: No, I don't currently have one.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: It was great meeting you, Alex. I really enjoyed our conversation today.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Before we wrap up, two quick things: where do you see yourself in three to five years, and is there anything else you'd like us to know about you? And what interests you most about this opportunity?

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: In three to five years, I'd like to be a senior analyst mentoring newer team members. I'd also like you to know that I'm a dedicated learner and I'm excited to bring my analytical skills to Northwind.

turns.speaker: candidate
turns.text: Wonderful. Thanks so much for your time today, Alex. We'll be in touch soon.
